title: AUTOEDIT AI-GPU-1 redacted acceptance evidence
description: Canonical redacted audit contract for OPS-AIGPU1-001..008, SEC-AIGPU1-002/003, and TEST-AIGPU1-005/007/008. Semantic rules in x-autoedit-semantic-rules are mandatory harness validation rules, not documentation-only annotations.

x-autoedit-semantic-rules.rule: A live action is forbidden unless discovery is complete, authorization.status is pass, explicit_peter_authorization is true, the current UTC time is inside the authorized window, and the exact host, fixture, actions, and cleanup resources are in authorization.scope.
Discovery, preparation, schema validation, mock execution, a prior authorization, or a human acceptance of code is never live authorization.
Discovery operations are allowlisted read-only operations. No health/readiness probe may start, restart, recreate, load, unload, or otherwise ensure a service.

Compose is rendered and parsed without up, start, run, restart, create, pull, build, or service mutation. Raw interpolated output is held in memory only; only the redacted canonical render and its digest may persist.

nominal_interval_ms is 1..250; scheduled tick spacing is <=250 ms; sequences and monotonic times strictly increase; observed gaps are positive and <=500 ms; every sample reconciles total=used+free and records process and service arrays.

Monotonic time is authoritative for order, duration, and overlap. Wall and monotonic elapsed time differ by no more than 500 ms across run anchors and phase markers; a backward or irreconcilable clock fails.

All eight phases exist exactly once, follow sequence 0..7 without overlap or inversion, and every sample belongs to the enclosing phase. baseline and dots_resident each last >=10000 ms; post_workload begins after both final workloads end and lasts >=30000 ms before cleanup.
Every GPU process row has an exact positive PID, allowlisted basename, non-negative per-process MiB, and approved attribution. Missing process accounting or any unknown process fails.
For active_overlap_1 and active_overlap_2, overlap_ms=min(end)-max(start) from actual inference intervals is >=5000 ms. Phase duration alone is not evidence.

Exactly three Whisper outputs cover cold, overlap 1, and overlap 2; each reaches done, matches the private input hash, and has non-empty ordered bounded aligned words plus two-speaker turns. Exactly two Dots outputs cover overlap 1 and overlap 2; each is non-empty and passes a playable-media probe. Raw content is absent.

App, worker, and Dots remain healthy; worker and Dots are ready when applicable; restart deltas are zero; Ollama loaded_model_count is zero before, throughout, and after measurement; no OOM, offload, eviction, readiness loss, queue overflow, or health loss occurs.

All samples use one device total. The global peak across all samples is reported with phase, sample, PID contributors, minimum free, and required headroom=max(2048,ceil(total_mib*0.10)); minimum_free_mib must be >= required_headroom_mib.

Only preauthorized temporary/opt-in resources may be changed. adjusted_drift_mib=post_cleanup_median-baseline_median-preauthorized_explained_mib and must be <=512; a resident exception must be declared before live activity. App health and both mock backend values pass after cleanup.

Any live mandatory fail, skipped, unavailable, or unauthorized result stops further workload submission, invokes only authorized cleanup/rollback, preserves prior artifacts/cuts, leaves or restores mock, reports the failed requirement, and exits non-zero. A retry never changes production defaults.

A live pass uses only the approved opaque secret-source reference and persists no secret values in evidence, argv, or public errors. Deep redaction runs before every write, stdout/stderr emission, exception message, and durable summary. Prohibited keys or values cause redaction_failure; unredacted raw command output is never retained.

A live pass requires non-null exact source commit, immutable worker image digest, canonical redacted Compose-render digest, runtime versions, opaque project/fixture/run references, FPS rational, automatic sync offsets, private input-hash match=true, and the fixed Whisper/Dots configurations. Discovery/mock placeholders cannot satisfy this rule.

pass means executed and met; fail means executed and breached; skipped means deliberately not executed; unavailable means an allowed dependency or observation was absent; unauthorized means permission was absent or out of scope. run.status equals overall.status; overall.failed_requirements exactly lists non-pass requirement_results. For a live run every mandatory check and requirement must be pass; all other states make overall.acceptance_pass false and exit non-zero.
A successful discovery-only or mock command may exit zero but must set acceptance_eligible=false and acceptance_pass=false. Only a fully passing authorized live run may set acceptance_pass=true.
Offline tests deny network and external commands by default, use injected fakes, assert schema validity, status, sanitized diagnostics, and non-zero exit for every required negative scenario.
